purpose: Issue #83: versioned catalog of LeanChecker.Kits modules — which kit to import, which canonical lemmas exist, which benchmark gaps each kit targets, and which fixtures prove it is alive. Metadata only: a registry entry never grants proof authority; kits provide tools, and the kernel decides outcomes.
usage: Add the module_path to problem_imports alongside canonical_open_directives, then reference key_lemmas by their fully qualified names. Each entry's prompt_block is a one-line context string suitable for an external prover.

kits.domain: synthetic Euclidean geometry — unoriented/oriented angle bridges
fixtures: 1965 A1 scalar endgame (linarith from the two kit equations)
1965 A1 Y-side scalar equation via three-ray decomposition
kits.prompt_block: GeometryAngleKit: isosceles base-angle and Sbtw supplementary bridges turn configuration hypotheses into scalar equations; three_ray_angle_decomposition (needs Fact finrank=2 + Module.Oriented instances) gives the universal three-rays-at-a-vertex constraint. Endgame is always linarith, never nlinarith over angle atoms.

kits.domain: affine plane geometry — cevian/Routh area ratios via coordinates
fixtures: one-seventh triangle (Routh k=2) area ratio by norm_num over coordinates
general-k cevian intersection with real segment membership
remaining_route_steps: extract A' = divPoint B C k from the benchmark's segment + dist-ratio hypotheses
affine normalization of a noncollinear triangle to the frame (0,0),(1,0),(0,1)
standard-triangle constant: volume (convexHull {(0,0),(1,0),(0,1)}) = 1/2 (or just 0 < V₀ < ∞ — the constant cancels in the benchmark ratio)
kits.prompt_block: AffineAreaKit: signed-area determinant, ratio-k division points, general-k cevian intersections, Routh inner determinant (k−1)²/(k²+k+1) for all real k, and the volume bridge volume(convexHull {a,b,c}) = ofReal |triangleDet| · V₀ (standard-triangle constant V₀ still uncomputed in Mathlib; it cancels in ratio problems).

fixtures: locally equal tsum power series have equal coefficients (benchmark-shaped phrasing)
raw ball hypotheses + radius bounds imply coefficient equality
Real.exp coefficients (n!)⁻¹ in the ∑ pₙxⁿ shape
remaining_route_steps: nonvanishing of exp(ax)cos(bx) near 0 → summability on a small ball → radius bound (bridges exist; assembly per-problem)
coefficient formula for exp(ax)*cos(bx): p n = re((a+bI)^n)/n! via the complex exponential
zero-set dichotomy from rationality/periodicity of arctan(b/a)/π
kits.prompt_block: PowerSeriesKit: pins an arbitrary coefficient sequence to THE power-series coefficients of f. v2 handles the raw benchmark hypothesis: summable_of_tsum_ne_zero (tsum≠0 forces summability), le_radius_ofScalars_of_summable_at (plain summability at x bounds radius), hasFPowerSeriesAt_of_eqOn_ball (ball equation → HasFPowerSeriesAt). Endgame: coeff_eq_of_hasFPowerSeriesAt.

kits.domain: finite planar convexity — Happy-Ending-style convex position
fixtures: unit square corners in convex position via explicit separating functionals
benchmark-shaped witness extraction: four distinct extreme points of the hull yield ∃ T ⊆ S, T.ncard = 4 ∧ ConvexPosition
remaining_route_steps: count extreme points of a 5-point hull with no 3 collinear (3/4/5 trichotomy)
hull = 3: two interior points plus two vertices on one side of their line (side-of-line case analysis + v1 separation criterion)
kits.prompt_block: FiniteConvexityKit: ConvexPosition in the benchmark's exact shape; v1 separation criterion (one functional per point) plus v2 extreme-point machinery — any 4 distinct extreme points of convexHull S give the 1962 A1 existential via convexPosition_of_subset_extremePoints + ncard_four. Extreme-point counting is the missing piece.

kits.domain: number theory — divisor sums, valuations, Pell recurrences, modular periodicity
kits.epistemic_basis: kernel_verified_only (no empirical or certificate-backed claims; certificate-backed finite search is issue #88's scope)
fixtures: σ₁(28) = 56 (perfect number) through the coprime factorization 4·7
last digit of 3^2026 via the period-4 modular cycle
Pell chain: (3,2) → (17,12) via the recurrence step

known_acceptance_targets.benchmark: Erdős-style number theory (no registered suite yet)

remaining_route_steps: Pell fundamental-solution existence (when a concrete target needs it)
kits.prompt_block: ArithmeticKit: divisor sums as σ bridges (sigma_one_eq_sum_divisors, sigma_mul_of_coprime for coprime splits), prime-power divisibility as factorization inequalities (prime_pow_dvd_iff), and Pell solution propagation (pell_step via linear_combination). Modular periodicity pattern: factor the exponent through the cycle length, then Nat.mul_mod/Nat.pow_mod.

kits.domain: extremal combinatorics — colorings, Ramsey/Schur, SAT certificates
kits.epistemic_basis: trust ladder (docs/kits/certificate-interface.md): rung 1 kernel decide (Schur S(2)=4 both directions, K5 pentagon witness, CNF fixtures), rung 2 verified-LRAT bv_decide (R(3,3) ≤ 6 — external CaDiCaL search, formally verified checker replay), rung 3 kernel-checked solver models (cnfSat_of_witness); rung 4 general LRAT import planned; rung 5 bare solver output is NEVER proof authority
fixtures: R(3,3) = 6 pinned both directions: pentagon witness by kernel decide, K6 upper bound by verified-LRAT bv_decide
Schur S(2) = 4 both directions by kernel decide
pigeonhole PHP(2,1) UNSAT by bounded kernel enumeration
solver-model import checked by kernel evaluation
edgeIdx range + injectivity sanity for K6

known_acceptance_targets.benchmark: Erdős-style finite combinatorics (no registered suite yet)
known_acceptance_targets.upstream_problem_id: Schur/Rado, small Ramsey bounds, EGZ instances, SAT-encodable extremal families

remaining_route_steps: rung 4: Cnf → Std.Sat.CNF bridge + satisfiability-preservation lemma, unlocking general LRAT import at CaDiCaL scale
per-family encoding-soundness lemmas (statement ↔ CNF encoding); interim pattern: state finite claims over BitVec/Bool so bv_decide applies directly
DIMACS export helper + certificate artifact hashing flow (report via issue #92 fields, files private)
kits.prompt_block: ExtremalCombinatoricsKit: encode K_n edge 2-colorings as BitVec (edgeIdx for the lexicographic pair order, monoBit for triangle checks); tiny searches close by kernel decide, larger ones by bv_decide (external SAT + formally verified LRAT replay — kernel-grade trust, see R(3,3) ≤ 6). CNF layer: cnfSat_of_witness kernel-checks solver models; cnfUnsat_of_forall_fin kernel-checks bounded UNSAT. Bare solver verdicts are never proof authority.
